name: John Zorn
abstract: John Zorn (born September 2, 1953) is an American composer, conductor, producer, arranger and saxophonist who "deliberately resists category". Zorn's avant-garde compositions and experimental improvisations meld jazz, rock, hardcore, classical, contemporary, surf, metal, soundtrack, ambient, Jewish and world music performed by "often une…

works_created.t: Aporias: Requia for Piano and Orchestra
Cat O'Nine Tails (Tex Avery Directs the Marquis de Sade)

about_quotes.speaker: Randy Sandke
about_quotes.evidence: I’ve noticed this with people like John Zorn, who’s been very successful... I like the group he’s got, Masada, with Dave Douglas. I love — I’ve heard some of his orchestral stuff. He’s a very talented guy.
about_quotes.doc_title: Randy Sandke interviewed by Monk Rowe, Aspen, Colorado, October 10, 1997

about_quotes.speaker: Phil Woods
about_quotes.evidence: I love what John Zorn is doing. ... John Zorn fits this category. ... This is my kind of guy.
about_quotes.doc_title: Phil Woods interviewed by Monk Rowe, Delaware Water Gap, PA, November 8, 1999

about_quotes.speaker: Ken Peplowski
about_quotes.evidence: the bass player I have in my band now, Greg Cohen, plays with John Zorn’s band also.
about_quotes.doc_title: Ken Peplowski interviewed by Monk Rowe, Aspen, Colorado, October 10, 1997 [interview 1 of 2]
